title: Ortgiesia caudata
taxonomy: Ortgiesia caudata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is a member of the tribe Scandiceae and is native to the southwestern part of South America. The plant is also known as Ortgiesia, a name that is derived from the Swiss botanist Paul Ortgies. The exact classification of the plant may be subject to change as new research emerges. The classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics.
uses: Ortgiesia caudata is a plant species native to Africa, and research suggests it may have potential medicinal uses, although further studies are necessary to confirm its efficacy.
